body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

body.title: DRIP Entity Tag Authentication Formats & Protocols for Broadcast Remote ID
DRIP Entity Tags in the Domain Name System
DRIP Authentication Formats for Broadcast RID & DET Registration & Lookup

tree there's a branch on the left that consists of the irm and the mra which is mostly dealing with serial numbers and then the branch on the right which deals with other raas mostly under civil aviation authorities and they have uh values of ras greater than two and hda is greater than one and then of course an unmanned aircraft would register with two different registries in the system one for its serial number and one for its session id and then with broadcast rate going to the observer and then dns and rdap lookups happening over the internet we have three major typical registration operations the serial number registration of the manufacturer into the mra and that's usually a d encoded as an ansi cta 2063-8 serial number and this is documented in the deep draft that bob has there's an operator registration at what is nominally would be considered a uss but it's actually specifically the remote id registry uh portion of drip and this key indeed would be used by the
